programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: Gibbs receives a call from his old friend Senator Patrick Kiley, who claims he is being framed for the murder of a naval commander, and seeks help to clear his name. However, incriminating evidence quickly piles up against him - and a second death complicates matters further. Meanwhile, Ziva lets her hair down to obtain fingerprints from a big oil lobbyist. Crime drama, starring Mark Harmon(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: A prostitute and what seems to be one of her clients are gunned down in a drive-by shooting, and when it emerges that the would-be John was actually a Navy officer, Gibbs and DiNozzo realise the killings are connected Lex Tarlionas, an incomplete operation to bring justice to those responsible for an attack on a US airport in 1979, which left 100 people dead. The main suspect is a former Colombian drugs kingpin known as Tomas Orlando, and the murdered sex worker was about to hand over crucial DNA evidence that could have connected him to the crime, but now that she is dead, Gibbs and the others must find another way to finally bring Orlando in. American drama, starring Mark Harmon and David McCallum(n)

programme.title: Matlock

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: The team investigates the death of a marine off the coast of Tanzania - a case complicated by the discovery that he had a link to Ziva. programme.title: CSI: NY
programme.desc: Taylor investigates the murder of a student who has been beaten and shot, and the discovery of heroin traces in his apartment leads him to suspect the killing was drug-related. The case becomes more urgent when it emerges that the victim's female room-mate has disappeared. Stella embarks on a harsh line of interrogation that results in her methods being called into question(n)

programme.title: NCIS
programme.desc: DiNozzo teams up with his girlfriend, ATF agent Zoe Keates, to investigate the circumstances surrounding the death of a US Marine in what initially seems like a simple road accident. The team reveals the dead man did indeed die due to a broken neck sustained during a crash, but someone shot at him first. As DiNozzo and Keates pursue their inquiry, they discover the victim's death is connected to a controversial ATF plan to place traceable firearms in the criminal system, and then follow them to the heads of crime organisations. Naval crime drama, starring Michael Weatherly(n)

programme.title: Bondi Rescue
programme.desc: A flash rip sweeps dozens of swimmers out of the flags and into deep water. Lifeguards are overwhelmed by the mass rescue, and one man is dragged unconscious from the water. The crowd mobs lifeguards treating a spinal as rescues continue in the water(n)

But a whole new can of worms is opened when investigators discover the lad's mother was raped - could the same person have killed Agnes? programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part one of two. Nikki is called in when a private jet crashes in a forest outside London, and finds herself forced to be the bearer of bad news when she learns that the casualties include a former US ambassador who is also an old friend of Matt's. Meanwhile, Thomas investigates the suspicious suicide of a successful businessman, and uncovers an unexpected connection to the plane crash - which suggests the two cases are linked, and that the plane may have been deliberately sabotaged as part of a coordinated attack(n)

programme.title: Veganville
programme.desc: Five vegans head to Merthyr Tydfil to try to convert local meat-eaters to a plant-based lifestyle, challenging views on both sides of the debate in the process. They set up a welcome event in the town centre, but while some of the group try to win the locals over with cake, another favours the hard-hitting approach of playing footage from abattoirs in the town centre(n)

programme.title: Snow Animals
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin introduces a cast of charismatic animals to reveal the remarkable strategies they use to survive, and even thrive, in the winter. Preparations start in autumn for Siberian chipmunks, which collect food in their cheek pouches to stockpile in their burrows, while caribou make an epic journey to new feeding areas away from the worst weather. With the arrival of winter, an American bobcat has to find its prey beneath deep snow, an arctic fox relies on its wits to find a meal, and a sea otter keeps its cub warm by blowing air into its coat(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ayesha teaches Al a lesson, and Bear visits Jimmi in prison. Meanwhile, an unidentified homeless man is found dead on a freezing street(n)

programme.title: Father Brown

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton make their way from Leicestershire to South Yorkshire, where the rivals try each other's tactics. Along the way, James discovers a local delicacy little known outside the region and Catherine hears the story of the most decorated soldier of the Great War - a man who never fired a gun(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Alex Jones and Patrick Kielty present(n)

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin help a family of fussy eaters in Gosport on the south coast lower their food bills and change their unhealthy eating habits. James and Rianna have two children, Tallulah-Belle who is three and Penelope who is one. Tallulah-Belle was born 11 weeks early weighing just one pound and now suffers with chronic lung disease. The couple spent over two years in hospital with her, living on convenience freezer food and takeaways, and now she is home their diets have not changed. Can Gregg and Chris help this family get their spending and eating habits back on track?(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part two of two. Nikki and the Lyell team continue to investigate the plane crash, ruling out an explosion and engine failure. The investigation takes a turn when they discover a connection to a sinister online forum. Meanwhile, Nikki's partner Matt is grieving following the death of his close friend, putting their relationship under strain. Starring Emilia Fox, David Caves and Richard Lintern(n)

programme.title: Eating with My Ex: Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Two famous celebrity ex-couples, Love Island's Michael Griffiths and Joanne Chiminodes, and TOWIE's Diags and Fran Parman, answer awkward questions about their failed relationships(n)

programme.title: My Mate's A Bad Date
programme.desc: Relationship expert Oloni helps seven disastrous daters, beginning with 21-year-old Kelsie from Newcastle, whose wild antics are keeping potential boyfriends away(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. Eleven contestants start each show and compete across a range of five play-along-at-home games that will see one of them leave with a cash prize of up to £10,000. Hosted by Jason Manford(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Charlie Stayt and Naga Munchetty present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville tell the story of travellers turned away from cruises because they were expecting a baby and the case of one unhappy couple who returned from their Caribbean wedding still legally unhitched. Plus, the bereaved families who found little sympathy from their travel companies(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell follow the development of a semi in Mottingham in south-east London, a detached in Littleover, Derby and a terrace in Dover in Kent(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Nicki Chapman profiles fireman Matt Parsons, who wants to move to Australia, but wife Jessica is not so keen. It remains to be seen whether a week in Melbourne will change her mind(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A shopkeeper grapples a gun from the hands of an armed robber, a retiree is duped on his own doorstep by a master scammer and a fraudster tries to claim compensation after a fall(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Experts Ben Cooper and Tim Weeks help two teams look for items at an antiques fair in Edinburgh, with presenter Natasha Raskin Sharp visiting the city's famous underground vaults(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Valerie takes samples of her DNA for testing, Joe has an eye infection and Karen is worried about Jimmi. Ayesha uncovers a secret while helping a school out with injections(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Murder proves to be no laughing matter when Father Brown visits a jesters' convention. Whodunit, starring Mark Williams as the sleuthing priest, with Neil Pearson and Paul Copley(n)

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jonnie Irwin revisits the Isle of Wight with a couple who originally had ambitions to carve out a new life for them and their son while tapping into the island's tourist trade(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two families promote their speciality cheeses and different forms of garlic at the Scottish Game Fair, having worked hard to get their products to market(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton travel across Derbyshire and Leicestershire, where items of interest including 18th-century carvings and a bowl full of marbles. Along the way Catherine gets steamed up on the heritage Great Central Railway and James visits a pottery(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Live magazine show featuring a mixture of celebrity chat and stories of interest. Alex Jones and Patrick Kielty present(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As Leo starts work on the market, Martin and Kush warn him to stay out of everyone's way. Gray quizzes Chantelle about Jags again, prompting her to keep her distance from the Panesars. Karen is angry with Honey when she hears Janet is not taking part in the pageant(n)

programme.title: Britain's Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry asks the contestants to produce their ultimate meringue dessert for a family celebration lunch - leading to stunning pavlovas, inventive fruit curd pies and even a woodland-themed roulade. This is followed by Chris Bavin's one-hour challenge to rustle up a dish from scratch featuring a humble vegetable as the star of the show, before the judges decide who has to take on Angela Hartnett's elimination cook for their place in the contest - a single recipe that requires precision timing and perfect seasoning. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The return of the whodunit set on the Caribbean island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. And so it appears this week as the locals set out to celebrate the New Year - only for the festivities to be cut short when a masked man stabs a woman in her own home, providing Detective Inspector Jack Mooney and his team with yet another murder to solve. Ardal O'Hanlon stars as the Irish detective - although as has been widely reported, he will soon be leaving the lead role - with guest appearances by Adrian Edmondson, Nina Wadia and Samuel West(n)

programme.title: Question Time
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Oxford, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.title: Brexitcast
programme.desc: The TV version of the popular radio show taking a light-hearted view of all things related to Brexit and beyond, with Laura Kuenssberg, Katya Adler, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason engaging in banter about the ongoing process to leave the EU(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Cassie Thorn is convinced moving to Australia will be perfect for her family, but her husband Aaron is not so sure, so they and their children sample life in Brisbane for a week(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Poultry farmers Nick and Jacob and organic and heritage fruit and vegetable farmer Patrick display their wares at Alexandra Palace Farmer's Market(n)

programme.title: MasterChef: The Professionals
programme.desc: The first heat of the week sees three chefs challenged to prepare a croque monsieur in 15 minutes for Marcus Wareing, while Monica Galetti sets three more the task of making pea risotto. The six contestants move on to the Signature Dish stage, where they must inspire both judges with their own unique creation before three are chosen to go through to the quarter final and three leave the contest(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Nicholas Crane tells the tale of the Great Storm of 1703, which saw ships blown from British shores to Norway, while Ian McMillan discusses the fate of hospital ship Rohilla, which ran aground off Whitby, North Yorkshire, in 1914. Dick Strawbridge visits Tiree in the Inner Hebrides, where he explains how the timings of the D-Day landings were determined by RAF weather forecasters(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: With the aid of a property expert, a Leeds duo hope to rent their house so they can spend winter in the Costa Blanca. Gloria Hunniford narrates(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of recipes to sate cravings for a takeaway, including fish and chips from James Martin and a pizza recipe from Keith Floyd(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Actor Charles Dance made his name playing aristocrats - a far cry from his own background, having been born to a parlour maid. He wants to know if he comes from a long line of servants or if he can uncover some grander origins. The actor is also determined to find out about his father, who died when he was four and about whom he knows hardly anything - a search that takes him to the other side of the world(n)

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: The land is bathed in 24-hour sunlight during the short Alaskan summer, luring hummingbirds up from Mexico, exposing deserts in the Arctic and relying on parachuting firefighters to tackle forest fires in its vast wilderness. Grizzly bears fatten up on a sudden wealth of salmon, a caribou calf joins a vast herd on its great migration and a huge male moose finds unlikely ways to impress a female. Among the human population, a dedicated grower of giant vegetables takes his half-ton pumpkin to the Alaska State Fair(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: At the Royal Marines Museum at Southsea in Hampshire, antiques expert Charles Hanson uncovers a navigational tool and Catherine Southon enthuses about a decorative Japanese cabinet dating back to the 1870s. Plus, Paul Martin finds out what it was like to serve as Royal Marine commando(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Gary Delaney, Rose Matafeo, Valerie Singleton and Rav Wilding go toe-to-toe testing their general knowledge skills in a variety of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo is in Northumbria en route to the Highlands, heading to Kielder Forest to discover what lay behind a national initiative to plant one of the largest manmade woodlands in Europe. Crossing the border to Scotland, Michael arrives in the weaving town of Hawick to visit Lovat Mill, where, in the 1930s, tweed was big business, and also goes to the movies, sitting in the front stalls at the Dominion cinema in Morningside(n)

programme.title: The Baby Has Landed
programme.desc: Documentary series following families throughout their first weeks of parenthood, lifting the lid on one of the most joyful, yet intense, times in a family's life. The opening episode introduces the first set of the six expectant families featured in the series, including young first-time parents Syler and Mo Nicholson-Bayoumi from Nottinghamshire, and Helen and Nigel Pierce, who are expecting their fourth child(n)

programme.title: Cornwall: This Fishing Life
programme.desc: Documentary about the Cornish fishing village of Mevagissey. While many fishing communities are struggling, this one has thrived owing to protection from prevailing winds and returning fish stocks. Boats have been handed down through generations and currently 74 operate out of the harbour. As young captains prepare to follow in their father's footsteps, tourism also becomes a major boost to the town - but leaves it feeling deserted in winter. A veteran skipper prepares to retire, and questions what the next generation will learn from his example(n)

programme.title: The Choir: Aylesbury Prison
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gareth desperately needs to expand his choir, with only seven inmates committing to the project and just three weeks before they have to put on a concert. He finds fresh hope in an encounter with a young man who has turned the story of his descent into crime into a song - and his example inspires others to share their own personal experiences. However, Gareth still needs voices, so tries to recruit some of the prison officers to help(n)

programme.title: Better Things
programme.desc: Sam helps Max settle in to her first days of college life in Chicago and has an unsettling return journey back to Los Angeles(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: NFL This Week
programme.desc: Mark Chapman is joined by Jason Bell and Osi Umenyiora to look back at the wild card play-offs(n)

programme.title: Murder, Mystery and My Family
programme.desc: Jeremy Dein and Sasha Wass return to tackle more potential miscarriages of justice as they re-examine historic murder cases on behalf of the family of those convicted. In the first edition they investigate their oldest case yet, the murder of a canal boat passenger 180 years ago in which boatmen were convicted and publicly hanged(n)

programme.title: Island Medics
programme.desc: Return of the programme following life at the Gilbert Bain Hospital in Lerwick, Shetland. A Norwegian sailor is brought in after fracturing his ankle aboard his ship, while new junior doctor Freddie Searight must dust off his French to treat an Indonesian fisherman who comes into A&E with an eye injury(n)

programme.title: This Is BBC Two
programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Property series in which people get the chance to experience life in Australia or New Zealand before deciding whether they want to make the move permanently(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two families of pig farmers are hoping to turn their dreams into a reality and win the top prizes with their precious livestock at the Royal Norfolk Show(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Nadeem Islam explores the culture of Wales, before sampling some of the country's adrenaline sports. With voiceover(n)

programme.title: Fake Britain
programme.desc: The story of an innocent holiday-maker locked up in a Dubai prison after unwittingly changing a fake £20 note, the fake dog harnesses putting the lives of pets at risk and the experts in Leeds separating real from fake designer clothing. Also, cameras follow Border Force officers intercepting potentially lethal medicines and members of the National Crime Agency as they raid the homes of currency counterfeiters. Presented by Dom Littlewood and Michelle Ackerley(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Update on politics from Westminster and beyond(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Exploring the tidal ebb and flow of the seas surrounding Britain. Nicholas Crane braves the dangerous rapids off the coast of Anglesey, and investigates a tidal predictor in Liverpool. Miranda Krestovnikoff visits Jersey's Seymour Tower to witness the marine life revealed at low spring tide, Tessa Dunlop compares the fashions in bathing suits favoured by different generations of beach-goers and Mark Horton travels to Poppit Sands, Pembrokeshire, to explore the remains of a 900-year old fish trap(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: Friends Tanya and Luisa want to swap the cold of Tyne and Wear for the sunshine of Ibiza, and are shown four rental properties on the Spanish island by local property expert, Julia(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott introduces a selection of old-school recipes. They include Keith Floyd's Irish stew, the Hairy Bikers' meatloaf, James Martin's ultimate bacon butty, a treacle tart from Mary Berry and Paul Hollywood, and a chili con carne from Lorraine Pascale(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Choreographer and Strictly judge Craig Revel Horwood becomes the latest celebrity to dig into his family's past in the popular genealogy show. Having lived and worked in Britain for the past 26 years, he now returns to his native Australia, curious to know if anyone else in the family was also involved in showbusiness. He discovers that one ancestor risked everything in the Australian gold rush, before a visit to his 100-year-old grandmother puts him on the trail of another maverick relative, who was the life and soul of the party. Sound like anyone we know?(n)

programme.title: Alaska: Earth's Frozen Kingdom
programme.desc: To survive the bone-chilling cold, deadly blizzards and darkness of the eight months of an Alaskan winter takes courage, cunning and remarkable endurance. This episode tells the stories of how the tough and resourceful animal and human inhabitants of the windswept mountain peaks, icy tundra and snowbound forests face up to the challenges of the season(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: People are invited to try to make money out of their antiques by taking a risk at auction(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Previous winners Gary Delaney, Rose Matafeo, Valerie Singleton and Rav Wilding test their knowledge in a series of trivia-based games across the week(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo reaches the Westerglen Transmitting Station in Falkirk, before visiting Gleneagles and Berry Town on route to his final stop on this leg, the city of Dundee(n)

programme.title: The Andrew Neil Show
programme.desc: Interviews, discussion and analysis from Westminster, including the latest on Brexit with Andrew Neil and guests(n)

programme.title: Saving Lives at Sea
programme.desc: The RNLI volunteers at Dunmore East in the Irish Sea are called to a motorboat stranded several miles out and taking on water, a man is caught under a pier in the Thames and two boys in north Wales get caught in a treacherous current chasing an errant football(n)

programme.title: Trust Me, I'm a Doctor
programme.desc: In the first episode of the series, medical journalist Michael Mosley and the team investigates how much exercise you really need to do to be healthy, and Alain Gregoire cures his lifelong phobia of heights in just two hours through the use of virtual reality. Elsewhere, Giles Yeo tests whether hot or cold drinks are better to cool you down(n)

programme.title: Lose Weight and Get Fit with Tom Kerridge
programme.desc: In this six-part series, the chef Tom Kerridge and a group of volunteers from Gloucester embark on an eight-week weight loss and fitness journey, doing regular exercise and eating lower calorie meals in an effort to get in shape and reach their health and fitness goals. In the first episode, Tom calls the group to a sports faculty at a local university to reveal what they have signed up to for the next two months(n)

programme.title: Murder Trial: The Disappearance of Margaret Fleming
programme.desc: Two-part documentary about the investigation and trial of two carers accused of murdering Margaret Fleming and claiming benefits in her name for 16 years, taking audiences to the centre of the trial at Glasgow's High Court and offering a compelling insight into the work of the police and prosecutors in bringing the two suspects to trial(n)

programme.title: The League of Gentlemen
programme.desc: Familiar faces come back to Royston Vasey to settle old scores and dig up old friends - with more bad blood than an abattoir with septicaemia. Reece Shearsmith, Steve Pemberton and Mark Gatiss star in scripts written by themselves and Jeremy Dyson(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Emily Maitlis(n)

programme.title: The Choir: Aylesbury Prison
programme.desc: Part one of two. Gareth Malone undertakes a challenge to form a choir in HMYOI Aylesbury, a prison for young offenders serving sentences from four years to life. First he meets Governor Laura Sapwell, who feels that a choir might be a different and unique way to engage with the young men as part of a programme aimed at trying to prevent people reoffending. At the end of his first week, Gareth has met almost all the prison's 200 residents, with 20 having expressed real interest in working with him(n)

programme.title: The Choir: Aylesbury Prison
programme.desc: Part two of two. Gareth desperately needs to expand his choir, with only seven inmates committing to the project and just three weeks before they have to put on a concert. He finds fresh hope in an encounter with a young man who has turned the story of his descent into crime into a song - and his example inspires others to share their own personal experiences. However, Gareth still needs voices, so tries to recruit some of the prison officers to help(n)

programme.title: See Hear
programme.desc: Nadeem Islam explores the culture of Wales, before sampling some of the country's adrenaline sports. With voiceover(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: An eco-friendly gift-wrap business gets a helping hand from Santa, and a sock entrepreneur embodies the holiday spirit with his buy-one-give-one business model. A pair of candle entrepreneurs also try to persuade the Dragons - Sara Davies, Touker Suleyman, Deborah Meaden, Tej Lalvani and Peter Jones - that their products can spark some serious profit. But while all the hopefuls dream of Dragon cash for Christmas, these investors don't give the gift of investment unless a business has real sparkle. Presented by Evan Davis(n)

programme.title: Dragons' Den
programme.desc: Angela Scanlon discovers what happened to some of the most memorable businesses and entrepreneurs who have exited the famous Den doors over the past 17 series. Angela takes Peter Jones, Deborah Meaden, Touker Suleyman, Tej Lalvani and new Dragon Sara Davies on a trip down memory lane when she shows them footage of themselves from former series, and she meets the latest, and youngest Dragon, Sara Davies, who has been a die-hard fan of the show since her university days(n)

programme.desc: Preview of upcoming programmes from BBC Two(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Rebecca, who previously lived in Australia, and her wife Rachel decide to spend a trial week living in down under to see if they should make the move there permanently(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Former police officer Huw Murphy and the Harries family go up against each other with their beloved shire horses at the 37th South Wales Shire Horse Society Show(n)

programme.title: Antiques Roadshow
programme.desc: Fiona Bruce looks back at memorable Roadshow items to find out what happened next, including what was really inside a 200-year-old bottle of wine that Andy McConnell tasted in 2016. Militaria specialist Mark Smith explores the mystery of a briefcase containing items that belonged to Second World War pilot Hubert Henderson, and unearths a remarkable story that takes in mistaken identity and the Great Escape. Asian art specialist Lee Young is reunited with the owners of a rare bronze water-dropper that was valued at £10,000 at a Roadshow in Newcastle and finds out what happened when it was put up for auction earlier this year(n)

programme.title: Victoria Derbyshire
programme.desc: Daily news and current-affairs programme offering discussion of breaking stories, exclusive interviews and audience interaction via social media(n)

programme.title: BBC Newsroom Live
programme.desc: A chance to stay up to date on the day's leading stories, with the latest breaking news as it happens(n)

programme.title: Politics Live
programme.desc: Andrew Neil and guests discuss the big political issues of the day(n)

programme.title: Coast
programme.desc: Exploring the stories and histories of Britain's beaches. Nicholas Crane searches for leeches in the pebble-pools of Dungeness in Kent, and learns how the area was formed. Hermione Cockburn takes to the air to view sand art in Jersey, Andy Torbet visits one of the nation's most dangerous beaches, Tessa Dunlop inspects a steelworks built on the shore of Port Talbot, Neath, and in Aberlady Bay, East Lothian, Nick Hewitt unearths submarines that played a pivotal role in sinking the Tirpitz, one of Hitler's largest battleships(n)

programme.title: Get Away for Winter
programme.desc: Mother and son Michelle and Jason from Southport want to spend the winter on Lanzarote - but they need a rental property matching two different tastes. Gloria Hunniford narrates(n)

programme.title: The Best Dishes Ever
programme.desc: Ainsley Harriott serves up a seafood special and his catches of the day include a fish pie from Mary Berry, a crab and leek tart from the Hairy Bikers, Nigel Slater's mackerel and bacon salad, a lobster dish from James Martin and Lorraine Pascale's fish and chips(n)

programme.title: Who Do You Think You Are?
programme.desc: Clare Balding is the latest celebrity to go in search of her roots, and she is particularly interested in her maternal great-grandfather. Clare believes he could have been gay, but getting to the truth of the matter proves a challenge when all the evidence comes from a time when homosexuality was illegal. Digging into her father's side of the family takes the sports presenter to New Jersey and New York where she uncovers an extraordinary dynasty and American roots stretching back generations(n)

programme.title: The Snow Wolf Family and Me
programme.desc: Part one of two. Wildlife cameraman Gordon Buchanan hopes to get closer to wild wolves than anyone before to discover the true nature of this feared predator and heads to the north of Canada in search of a pack that has never seen people and so has no fear of humans. He begins to gain the trust of an Arctic wolf family and as time passes becomes accepted by the pack, including a female and its three newborn pups, and has some close encounters that reveal a softer side to the animals(n)

programme.title: Flog It!
programme.desc: Paul Martin presents a special edition, revisiting some of the historic venues from the series' history. Paul also finds out about the scandalous Georgians of Lydiard Park(n)

programme.title: Richard Osman's House of Games
programme.desc: Gary Delaney, Rose Matafeo, Valerie Singleton and Rav Wilding test their general knowledge skills in trivia-based games, with the ultimate winner declared on Friday(n)

programme.title: Great British Railway Journeys
programme.desc: Michael Portillo travels from Dundee to Aberdeen, stopping off at Glamis Castle, where the Queen Mother grew up, and making an excursion into the Eastern Highlands(n)

programme.title: The Baby Has Landed
programme.desc: Documentary series following families throughout their first weeks of parenthood, lifting the lid on one of the most joyful, yet intense, times in a family's life. In the second episode, cameras follow new parents Mo and Syler as they get to grips with round-the-clock feeding and sleep deprivation, and Paul and Craig's surrogate Mel gives birth to their twins. Elsewhere, five-year-old Ezrah and seven-year-old Elijah have to come to terms with new rules in the family home after the birth of their baby sister(n)

programme.title: Baby Chimp Rescue
programme.desc: Documentary following life in a home for orphaned chimpanzees run by wildlife vet Jimmy Desmond and his wife Jenny. The Desmonds started with just two rescued baby chimps, saved from the illegal pet trade, but now their home is bursting at the seams with 21 orphans to look after. Their dream is to build them all a new sanctuary in the forest and, with help from friend and chimp specialist Ben Garrod, they teach the orphans the skills they would have learnt from their mothers in the wild(n)

programme.title: Murder Trial: The Disappearance of Margaret Fleming
programme.desc: Part two of two. The trial of Edward Cairney and Avril Jones continues, including the testimonies of Jean McSherry, the former partner of Margaret's father Derick, and Margaret's former teacher Jacqueline Cahill. The programme also follows senior investigating officer Detective Superintendent Paul Livingstone as he revisits Margaret's derelict home in Seacroft, Inverkip(n)

programme.title: The League of Gentlemen
programme.desc: Royston Vasey faces a situation more terrible than anything it has faced before as boundary changes threaten to erase the town from the map for ever. The fight to save the community from administrative annihilation comes from unexpected and surprising directions, all of them local. Comedy, starring Reece Shearsmith, Steve Pemberton and Mark Gatiss(n)

programme.title: Newsnight
programme.desc: Analysis of the day's events, presented by Kirsty Wark(n)

programme.title: The League of Gentlemen - Live Again!
programme.desc: A filmed performance of the comedy group's 2018 live show from London's Eventim Apollo, featuring Mark Gatiss, Steve Pemberton and Reece Shearsmith as popular characters including Papa Lazarou, Edward and Tubbs, and Dr Chinnery. The show picks up where the 2017 Christmas special left off in the depths of the dreaded 'Wife Mine', and also features Mickey and Pauline sharing a bed on stage, an ambitious new production from the Legz Akimbo theatre troupe, and some audience participation with Herr Lipp(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world. With Katty Kay and Christian Fraser(n)

programme.title: Monkman & Seagull's Genius Guide to Britain
programme.desc: University Challenge icons Eric Monkman and Bobby Seagull take a road trip with a difference, searching Scotland, England, Northern Ireland and Wales for hidden gems of British scientific and technological ingenuity. They begin with a trip to Blackpool Pleasure Beach to experience Sir Hiram Maxim's 1904 ‘Captive Flying Machines', followed by a visit to Southport, home to the only lawnmower museum of its kind in the world(n)

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Using the latest 3D scanning technology, Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott uncover the hidden history of three Italian cities, beginning with Naples, from its earliest Greek and Roman origins. The pair explore the impact of the volcano of Mount Vesuvius, and how it both nurtured the region and exacted a terrible price on the local population. Alexander and Michael also delve into a labyrinth of underground spaces constructed by ruling Bourbon kings in the 19th century, that helped to sustain the city(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Part one of two. The story of 1970s cult leader Jim Jones. The series explores how he was forced to flee the US, establishing a settlement in a remote part of Guyana, and the violent end of the project in 1978. After cult members opened fire on a US delegation, killing five people, Jones persuaded 918 of his followers to join him in mass suicide - including 304 children(n)

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett examines the impact of the conquest of Britain and Ireland, and learns how William the Conqueror built scores of castles and cathedrals to impose his control and intimidate the population. The ruler also commissioned the Domesday Book to survey the wealth of the land and raise revenue, and set in motion the linguistic and cultural transformation of England. The Normans blended well in Scotland, but faced fierce opposition in Wales, and promoted divisions in Ireland which some claim still exist to this day(n)

programme.title: Dan Cruickshank: At Home with the British
programme.desc: The historian celebrates British homes, starting with a visit to the picturesque Warwickshire countryside and the village of Stoneleigh, which has barely changed in 500 years, its cottages perfectly preserved. Here he charts the cottage's transformation from humble hovel to modern dream home(n)

programme.title: Knights of Classic Drama at the BBC
programme.desc: Archive footage of some of Britain's most acclaimed acting greats appearing in BBC drama at early stages of their careers. Featuring Michael Caine in a rare Shakespearean role, Ben Kingsley, Ian McKellen, Derek Jacobi and Michael Gambon, as well as behind the scenes footage of famous series including War and Peace, the Mayor of Casterbridge and the Singing Detective. Narrated by David Tennant(n)

programme.title: Big Sky, Big Dreams, Big Art: Made in the USA
programme.desc: Waldemar Januszczak examines the golden age of American art, beginning in the Wild West with the art of the Hudson River School, before culminating in a celebration of the work of Jackson Pollock, a native of Wyoming who was heavily influenced by the mood of the American West and the example of Native American artists(n)

programme.title: Italy's Invisible Cities
programme.desc: Using the latest 3D scanning technology, Alexander Armstrong and Dr Michael Scott uncover the hidden history of three Italian cities, beginning with Naples, from its earliest Greek and Roman origins. The pair explore the impact of the volcano of Mount Vesuvius, and how it both nurtured the region and exacted a terrible price on the local population. Alexander and Michael also delve into a labyrinth of underground spaces constructed by ruling Bourbon kings in the 19th century, that helped to sustain the city(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: James May: The Reassembler
programme.desc: James reassembles the 135 parts that make up a 1960s Kenwood Chef a701a food mixer, which revolutionised cooking and saved time for millions of people(n)

programme.title: Earth's Great Rivers
programme.desc: Documentary following three of the world's largest rivers, beginning with the Amazon, uncovering some of the latest discoveries, including the 'Blue Lagoon' which is home to a newly discovered species of cichlid. The programme also explores some of the Amazon's flooded forests, home to an array of unusual animals, and cameras follow giant river otters on a fishing foray on the rocky terraces of the Cristalino river(n)

programme.title: Storyville
programme.desc: Part two of two. Unreleased recordings, photographs taken by members of the People's Temple, previously classified FBI documents and new testimony from survivors reveal how and why this tragedy happened. The Jonestown project quickly begins to implode. Meanwhile, Jones's excessive drug use, irrational behaviour and the isolation of his followers raises the alarm back home(n)

programme.title: The Normans
programme.desc: Professor Robert Bartlett examines the Normans' expansion toward southern Europe and the Middle East in the 11th century. Southern Italy and Sicily were conquered and a prosperous kingdom flourished on the island, which became an important centre of medieval culture and an example of harmony between religions. However, Pope Urban II's call for holy war against the Muslims, to win Jerusalem back, led to the Normans embarking on the First Crusade(n)

programme.title: Treasures of Ancient Egypt
programme.desc: Alastair Sooke tells the story of Ancient Egyptian art through 30 masterpieces. He treks across the Sahara and travels along the Nile to find the rarely seen pieces crafted by the country's earliest inhabitants, and explores how the works often reflected people's religion. He is also joined by contemporary artists who are confounded by the technical skills of the craftsmen(n)

programme.title: Knights of Classic Drama at the BBC
programme.desc: Part two of two. Archive footage of some of Britain's most acclaimed acting legends appearing in BBC drama at early stages of their careers. The programme features early roles and some career-defining moments for Judi Dench, Maggie Smith, Diana Rigg and Helen Mirren. Narrated by David Tennant(n)

programme.title: Arena
programme.desc: The second of three programmes exploring American roots music focuses on gospel, including the story of a South Carolina religious leader who inspired Dizzy Gillespie with his use of music in worship. The programme also examines the music originating in the Mississippi Delta and the coal mining communities of Logan County, West Virginia(n)

programme.title: Earth's Great Rivers
programme.desc: Documentary following three of the world's largest rivers, beginning with the Amazon, uncovering some of the latest discoveries, including the 'Blue Lagoon' which is home to a newly discovered species of cichlid. The programme also explores some of the Amazon's flooded forests, home to an array of unusual animals, and cameras follow giant river otters on a fishing foray on the rocky terraces of the Cristalino river(n)

programme.title: Beyond 100 Days
programme.desc: BBC News teams in Washington DC and London report on the events that are shaping the world(n)

programme.title: A Slow Odyssey
programme.desc: An immersive journey into the world of a green sea turtle, using only natural sounds and embedded graphics to deliver detailed information about its daily routine(n)

programme.title: The Great British Year
programme.desc: Documentary following British wildlife through the seasons, beginning on New Year's Day, with the landscape in the grip of winter. Time-lapse photography shows a magical country shrouded in frost and swirling mist, while water becomes the enemy as the creatures must cope with ice. Red squirrels resort to subterfuge, kites track a farmer's plough to get at the worms beneath the frost, and as winter fades adders bask in the sun and the woodland floor erupts with snowdrops(n)

programme.title: Gravity and Me: The Force That Shapes Our Lives
programme.desc: Jim Al-Khalili investigates the amazing science of gravity, recreating groundbreaking experiments, including the moment when Galileo first worked out how to measure it. He investigates gravitational waves, finds out from astronauts what it's like to live without gravity, sets out to find where in Britain gravity is weakest and so where we weigh the least, and helps design a smartphone app that volunteers use to demonstrate how gravity affects time and makes us age at slightly different rates(n)

programme.title: Horizon
programme.desc: Examining Dark Energy, the mysterious force that is unexpectedly causing the universe's expansion to speed up. Its effects were discovered in 1998, but physicists still do not know what it is, and its very existence calls into question Albert Einstein's General Theory of Relativity; the cornerstone of modern physics. The hunt for the identity of Dark Energy is on, and although experiments conducted on earth and in space generate data that might provide a clue, physics is hoping another Einstein might emerge and write a new theory that explains the mystery(n)

programme.title: The Joy of Winning
programme.desc: A witty, mind-expanding guide in which Hannah Fry unlocks the essential strategies a person needs to get what they want - to win more of the time. The mathematician also hails 20th-century scientists John von Neumann and John Nash, who worked out the science of success and reveals how the same rules apply to the world's biggest problems - whether its avoiding nuclear annihilation or tackling climate change(n)

programme.title: EastEnders

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part one of two. Nikki is called in when a private jet crashes in a forest outside London, and finds herself forced to be the bearer of bad news when she learns that the casualties include a former US ambassador who is also an old friend of Matt's. Meanwhile, Thomas investigates the suspicious suicide of a successful businessman, and uncovers an unexpected connection to the plane crash - which suggests the two cases are linked, and that the plane may have been deliberately sabotaged as part of a coordinated attack(n)

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce from The Call Centre explores the issues that are affecting her generation, beginning by attempting to go sober for four weeks. Like many people her age, 29-year-old Hayley Pearce wouldn't dream of a night out without alcohol, but with recent studies revealing that nearly a third of 16-24 year olds now don't drink at all, Hayley wants to understand what's behind the growing tee-total trend among young people. To find out, she attempts to go sober for four weeks(n)

programme.title: Hayley Goes...
programme.desc: Hayley Pearce tests out her maternal side as she explores what it means to be a 'mam' for two weeks, undertaking midwife training and looking after a simulation baby(n)

programme.title: Snow Animals
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin introduces a cast of charismatic animals to reveal the remarkable strategies they use to survive, and even thrive, in the winter. Preparations start in autumn for Siberian chipmunks, which collect food in their cheek pouches to stockpile in their burrows, while caribou make an epic journey to new feeding areas away from the worst weather. With the arrival of winter, an American bobcat has to find its prey beneath deep snow, an arctic fox relies on its wits to find a meal, and a sea otter keeps its cub warm by blowing air into its coat(n)

programme.title: The One Show

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin help a family of fussy eaters in Gosport on the south coast lower their food bills and change their unhealthy eating habits. James and Rianna have two children, Tallulah-Belle who is three and Penelope who is one. Tallulah-Belle was born 11 weeks early weighing just one pound and now suffers with chronic lung disease. The couple spent over two years in hospital with her, living on convenience freezer food and takeaways, and now she is home their diets have not changed. Can Gregg and Chris help this family get their spending and eating habits back on track?(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part two of two. Nikki and the Lyell team continue to investigate the plane crash, ruling out an explosion and engine failure. The investigation takes a turn when they discover a connection to a sinister online forum. Meanwhile, Nikki's partner Matt is grieving following the death of his close friend, putting their relationship under strain. Starring Emilia Fox, David Caves and Richard Lintern(n)

programme.title: Battling for My Baby's Brain
programme.desc: Documentary about Tracy Jenkins' year-long mission to find treatment to enable her three-year-old son Hartley, who has cerebral palsy, to gain better control of his legs, arm and hands. The film follows her as she devotes every available moment to training Hartley's brain to build new connections, and also as she tracks down clinics and physiotherapists who can provide the latest treatments. Tracy also speaks to other people who have cerebral palsy to find out about their struggles to get help(n)

programme.title: Britain's Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry asks the contestants to produce their ultimate meringue dessert for a family celebration lunch - leading to stunning pavlovas, inventive fruit curd pies and even a woodland-themed roulade. This is followed by Chris Bavin's one-hour challenge to rustle up a dish from scratch featuring a humble vegetable as the star of the show, before the judges decide who has to take on Angela Hartnett's elimination cook for their place in the contest - a single recipe that requires precision timing and perfect seasoning. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The return of the whodunit set on the Caribbean island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. And so it appears this week as the locals set out to celebrate the New Year - only for the festivities to be cut short when a masked man stabs a woman in her own home, providing Detective Inspector Jack Mooney and his team with yet another murder to solve. Ardal O'Hanlon stars as the Irish detective - although as has been widely reported, he will soon be leaving the lead role - with guest appearances by Adrian Edmondson, Nina Wadia and Samuel West(n)

programme.desc: Fiona Bruce presents the topical debate from Oxford, inviting the studio audience to question a panel of politicians and other guests on subjects that have made the headlines over the past week(n)

programme.desc: Angela Rippon, Gloria Hunniford and Julia Somerville present stories of tourists who were left stranded by airlines - including a look at how insurance is not always a protection(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: A period flat in Folkestone, a three-bed semi in Middleton near Manchester and a bungalow in Derby are all sold at auction. With Martin Roberts, Dion Dublin and Martel Maxwell(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: The Goodinson Family samples life in New Zealand for a week to decide if they want to make a permanent move - but health concerns mean time is not on their side(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A medical student tracks down the hit-and-run driver who has left him for dead, a thief is tempted by a slab of fudge, and a wildlife camera catches burglars in the act(n)

programme.title: Bargain Hunt
programme.desc: Kate Bliss and Thomas Plant help two teams in Builth Wells, Powys, and presenter Natasha Raskin finds out about the history of Cardiff Bay with local expert James Cowen(n)

programme.desc: Local news worth watching from across Northern Ireland(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Al and Emma try to hatch a plan to free Jimmi, but Rob delivers a warning. Sid treats a prison officer who has been advised by Jimmi on the inside(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: The sleuthing priest investigates the mysterious death of a beekeeper. Nigel Planer guest stars in the 1950s-set murder mystery series(n)

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Two families of pig farmers are hoping to turn their dreams into a reality and win the top prizes with their precious livestock at the Royal Norfolk Show(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton embark on the second leg of their five-part expedition, heading to Worcestershire, Warwickshire and the West Midlands(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Another mix of nationwide reports and live studio-based chat. Presented by Alex Jones and Patrick Kielty(n)

programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: Gray is frustrated when Chantelle cannot give him the news he wants - and later, to make matters worse, he is unimpressed to see his wife talking to Jags in the Square. Ben has some harsh words for Louise when she berates him for talking about Sharon. Whitney is shocked to hear Leo is opening a stall on the market, tensions rise between Karen and Billy, and Jean is unimpressed when Mo suggests the Slaters should hold a seance to speak to Daniel(n)

programme.title: Holby City
programme.desc: Jac is discharged from the psychiatric unit, having made progress with her recovery - so is able to help a troubled Kian as he battles demons of his own. Max encounters a face from the past and is blackmailed into performing a dangerous surgery - if she refuses, her most painful secrets will be revealed to the world(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part one of two. Nikki is called in when a private jet crashes in a forest outside London, and finds herself forced to be the bearer of bad news when she learns that the casualties include a former US ambassador who is also an old friend of Matt's. Meanwhile, Thomas investigates the suspicious suicide of a successful businessman, and uncovers an unexpected connection to the plane crash - which suggests the two cases are linked, and that the plane may have been deliberately sabotaged as part of a coordinated attack(n)

programme.title: Veganville
programme.desc: Five vegans head to Merthyr Tydfil to try to convert local meat-eaters to a plant-based lifestyle, challenging views on both sides of the debate in the process. They set up a welcome event in the town centre, but while some of the group try to win the locals over with cake, another favours the hard-hitting approach of playing footage from abattoirs in the town centre(n)

programme.title: Snow Animals
programme.desc: Liz Bonnin introduces a cast of charismatic animals to reveal the remarkable strategies they use to survive, and even thrive, in the winter. Preparations start in autumn for Siberian chipmunks, which collect food in their cheek pouches to stockpile in their burrows, while caribou make an epic journey to new feeding areas away from the worst weather. With the arrival of winter, an American bobcat has to find its prey beneath deep snow, an arctic fox relies on its wits to find a meal, and a sea otter keeps its cub warm by blowing air into its coat(n)

programme.title: Breakfast
programme.desc: Dan Walker and Louise Minchin present a round-up of national and international news, plus current affairs, arts and entertainment, and weather(n)

programme.title: Rip Off Britain: Holidays
programme.desc: A report on the airlines refusing to let passengers fly because their passports are damaged. Plus, a man recalls his experience of being unfairly denied a hotel refund(n)

programme.title: Homes Under the Hammer
programme.desc: Martel Maxwell and Dion Dublin follow the stories of renovators who purchase a flat in Margate, Kent, a house in Romford, east London and a plot of land in Colne, Lancashire(n)

programme.title: Wanted Down Under
programme.desc: Rebecca, who previously lived in Australia, and her wife Rachel decide to spend a trial week living in down under to see if they should make the move there permanently(n)

programme.title: Caught Red Handed
programme.desc: A window cleaner is suspected when money goes missing from an elderly woman with dementia, and a burglar falls asleep at the scene of his crime. Presented by Dominic Littlewood(n)

programme.title: Doctors
programme.desc: Ayesha teaches Al a lesson, and Bear visits Jimmi in prison. Meanwhile, an unidentified homeless man is found dead on a freezing street(n)

programme.title: Father Brown
programme.desc: Father Brown must race to save Bunty from the hangman's noose when she is tried for murder. Fifties-set crime drama based, starring Mark Williams(n)

programme.title: I Escaped to the Country
programme.desc: Jules Hudson heads close to Loch Ness in the Scottish Highlands and is stunned by what his buyers with £375,000 have achieved with their stylish property(n)

programme.title: The Farmers' Country Showdown
programme.desc: Former police officer Huw Murphy and the Harries family go up against each other with their beloved shire horses at the 37th South Wales Shire Horse Society Show(n)

programme.title: Antiques Road Trip
programme.desc: Catherine Southon and James Braxton make their way from Leicestershire to South Yorkshire, where the rivals try each other's tactics. Along the way, James discovers a local delicacy little known outside the region and Catherine hears the story of the most decorated soldier of the Great War - a man who never fired a gun(n)

programme.title: Pointless
programme.desc: Quiz in which contestants try to score the fewest points possible by giving the least obvious correct answers to questions. Presented by Alexander Armstrong, with Richard Osman(n)

programme.title: The One Show
programme.desc: Magazine show, with stories of interest from around the UK, plus celebrity guests in the studio. Alex Jones and Patrick Kielty present(n)

programme.title: Eat Well for Less?
programme.desc: Gregg Wallace and Chris Bavin help a family of fussy eaters in Gosport on the south coast lower their food bills and change their unhealthy eating habits. James and Rianna have two children, Tallulah-Belle who is three and Penelope who is one. Tallulah-Belle was born 11 weeks early weighing just one pound and now suffers with chronic lung disease. The couple spent over two years in hospital with her, living on convenience freezer food and takeaways, and now she is home their diets have not changed. Can Gregg and Chris help this family get their spending and eating habits back on track?(n)

programme.title: Silent Witness
programme.desc: Part two of two. Nikki and the Lyell team continue to investigate the plane crash, ruling out an explosion and engine failure. The investigation takes a turn when they discover a connection to a sinister online forum. Meanwhile, Nikki's partner Matt is grieving following the death of his close friend, putting their relationship under strain. Starring Emilia Fox, David Caves and Richard Lintern(n)

programme.title: Eating with My Ex: Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Two famous celebrity ex-couples, Love Island's Michael Griffiths and Joanne Chiminodes, and TOWIE's Diags and Fran Parman, answer awkward questions about their failed relationships(n)

programme.title: My Mate's A Bad Date
programme.desc: Relationship expert Oloni helps seven disastrous daters, beginning with 21-year-old Kelsie from Newcastle, whose wild antics are keeping potential boyfriends away(n)

programme.title: First & Last
programme.desc: Game show where contestants have to make sure they do not come first or last in any game or they will be eliminated. programme.title: EastEnders
programme.desc: As Leo starts work on the market, Martin and Kush warn him to stay out of everyone's way. Gray quizzes Chantelle about Jags again, prompting her to keep her distance from the Panesars. Karen is angry with Honey when she hears Janet is not taking part in the pageant(n)

programme.title: Britain's Best Home Cook
programme.desc: Mary Berry asks the contestants to produce their ultimate meringue dessert for a family celebration lunch - leading to stunning pavlovas, inventive fruit curd pies and even a woodland-themed roulade. This is followed by Chris Bavin's one-hour challenge to rustle up a dish from scratch featuring a humble vegetable as the star of the show, before the judges decide who has to take on Angela Hartnett's elimination cook for their place in the contest - a single recipe that requires precision timing and perfect seasoning. Claudia Winkleman hosts(n)

programme.title: Death in Paradise
programme.desc: The return of the whodunit set on the Caribbean island of Saint Marie, where the sun shines all day and it seems half the residents are killers-in-waiting. And so it appears this week as the locals set out to celebrate the New Year - only for the festivities to be cut short when a masked man stabs a woman in her own home, providing Detective Inspector Jack Mooney and his team with yet another murder to solve. Ardal O'Hanlon stars as the Irish detective - although as has been widely reported, he will soon be leaving the lead role - with guest appearances by Adrian Edmondson, Nina Wadia and Samuel West(n)

programme.title: Murchadh MacPharlain: Bard Mhealaboist
programme.desc: Award-winning documentary profiling Murdo MacFarlane, the Melbost Bard, the renowned Gaelic poet and songwriter from the island of Lewis, whose work was a catalyst for a whole new era of modern Gaelic music. Having composed poems and songs all his life, it was not until the advent of the tape recorder that Murdo, who could neither read nor write music, became known to a wider audience. The programme contains TV and radio archive recordings of Murdo in interviews, and also of him performing his work(n)

programme.title: Sulaisgeir
programme.desc: For centuries, men from Ness in the Isle of Lewis in the Western Isles have sailed to a tiny island, 40 miles North of Lewis each summer to collect their harvest of seabirds. Dods MacFarlane has followed in the footsteps of his forefathers for over 40 years, but here he leads the annual guga hunt for the last time(n)

programme.title: Tipping Point
programme.desc: Ben Shephard hosts the quiz in which contestants answer questions to win turns on an arcade-style machine. Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: Britain's Busiest Motorway

Back in the control room, Emily spots some loose horses causing chaos at Junction 23, while at junction 10 a bridge joint is being replaced and the engineers are in a race against time to get the job done before the sun rises(n)

programme.title: The Martin Lewis Money Show: Live
programme.desc: The presenter provides an annual financial health check for the nation in the wake of the Christmas expenses, offering up some 'Bill-Busting' advice on the best ways to cut the costs of energy, broadband, mobile phones, and digital TV bills. Plus, more questions from the audience in Ask Martin Anything(n)

programme.title: The National Television Awards Celebrate 25 Years
programme.desc: Julie Walters narrates a look back at the long-running awards show. Previous hosts Trevor McDonald and Dermot O'Leary share their memories of anchoring the event, while their successor, David Walliams, looks ahead to taking the reins for the 2020 show. Among the stars sharing their memories of past awards ceremonies are Graham Norton, Ant & Dec, Holly Willoughby, David Tennant, Simon Cowell, Paul O'Grady, Richard & Judy, Danny Dyer, Craig Revel Horwood, Motsi Mabuse, Brendan O'Carroll, Phillip Schofield, Julie Hesmondhalgh, Michael Barrymore, Eamonn Holmes and Davina McCall(n)

programme.title: Piers Morgan's Life Stories
programme.desc: Timothy West joins the host to look back on a five-decade career as one of Britain's best-loved actors. The former EastEnders star speaks candidly about his 52-year marriage to Fawlty Towers actress Prunella Scales, and the impact of her battle with dementia on their lives. He also reveals the trauma of his first wife's mental health problems, and how he finally made peace with his daughter after several years of estrangement. On a lighter note, Timothy also reflects on his role at the wedding of Prince Charles and Camilla Parker-Bowles, and the time he toured the set of Coronation Street - disguised as former Soviet president Mikhail Gorbachev(n)

programme.desc: Quiz hosted by Warwick Davis in which five members of a running club answer questions about top 10 lists from the realms of pop culture and general knowledge, then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money(n)

programme.desc: Homeowners compete to make the biggest profit from a major renovation, while interior design expert Linda Barker monitors their progress and appraises the end results. programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor Bradley Walsh and his son Barney return for a second series as they embark on another road trip across America together, beginning their journey in Florida, the southernmost point of the country. From there, the pair head to the Palm Beach International Raceway, before getting up close and personal with sharks(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiz and Tyrone are questioned about the Christmas Day shootings. Meanwhile, Dev orders Chesney to make a choice, and Tracy's explanation shocks Leanne(n)

programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: Six-part drama based on the true story of the mass murder of a family in an Essex farmhouse in August 1985, providing a fresh insight into these tragic events. In the early hours of August 7th 1985, Nevill and June Bamber were shot and killed inside their farmhouse, along with their adoptive daughter, Sheila Caffell, and Sheila's six-year-old twin sons, Daniel and Nicholas Caffell. Police receive a call from the only surviving member of June and Nevill's immediate family, their adoptive son Jeremy. At first, it appears to be an open-and-shut case to investigating officer DCI Thomas 'Taff' Jones, but for DS Stan Jones, something does not feel right(n)

programme.title: Holiday Horrors: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Another selection of clips showcasing what can go wrong on holiday, including an unexpected visitor to a couple's tent on their safari holiday, footage from a GoPro camera lost at sea, and a man who took a flight to the edge of space(n)

programme.title: Through the Keyhole
programme.desc: Comedian and matchmaking game show host Paddy McGuinness, MasterChef's Gregg Wallace, and former Gogglebox star and one-time Queen of the Jungle Scarlett Moffatt form the panel this week, as Keith Lemon ventures unbidden into another three mystery celebrities' homes. Keith explores the residences pointing out tell-tale clues, before challenging his trio of stars to guess 'who habitates in a house like this'(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Traci, Sharon, Rodney and Jamie pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser. Answering general knowledge questions, they each add as much money as they dare to the jackpot - but anyone caught by the Chaser is out for good, leaving fewer players to team up and play in the final chase(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Pete is frustrated by Moira's behaviour, Graham receives a troubling call, and David and Leyla are troubled(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: Julie Etchingham explores the growing issue of 'cyber-stalking'. She asks what can be done to prevent technology being used in this way and talks to cyber-crime experts to find out how easy it is for an abuser to take control of their partner's technology(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dawn is blind-sided, Moira receives some harsh truths, and Sarah has a plan(n)

programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo's latest leg begins in the Italian fashion capital of Milan, where he visits an elegant cocktail bar who were the first to introduce the idea of the aperitivo in the city. Its train station is one of the busiest in Europe and it's from here he travels on to the town of Treviglio, the home of Bianchi bikes - the first mass-manufactured bicycle in the world. Gino gets a tour of the factory before test riding one of their latest electric bikes alongside a group of local cyclists. Moving on, he visits the medieval town of Bergamo, where he meets ice-cream maker Niccolo, and visits a co-operative restaurant(n)

programme.title: Welcome to HMP Belmarsh with Ross Kemp
programme.desc: The documentary maker goes inside the walls of HMP Belmarsh, the country's most notorious maximum-security jail, which has housed the country's most dangerous - and infamous - convicts, including Ian Huntley, Ronnie Biggs and Charles Bronson. Ross explores how staff cope with high-profile inmates, extremists and common criminals living side-by-side and goes inside the High Security Unit, where he meets Muhammad Asif Hafeez, known as Sultan, the alleged mastermind of a drug-smuggling empire(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the airport. Heavy fog throws Heathrow into turmoil and new terminal manager Callum is faced with hundreds of angry and stranded passengers stuck overnight at the airport, while visibility has dropped to less than 100m on the airfield and safety officer Chris is forced to marshal planes. Manager Nilam reveals the secrets of one of Heathrow's most exclusive first-class lounges and dispatcher James and his team hunt around the terminal for late passengers for a flight to Pakistan(n)

Dropping tokens down a choice of four chutes, they hope to knock piles of them off a moving shelf - and the more they collect, the greater the prize fund. The player who has won the least amount is then eliminated, and the last one standing competes for a £10,000 jackpot(n)

programme.title: The Chase
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as contestants Liz, Tariq, Sue and Tom pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiz gets caught breaking the rules, while Chesney and Gemma take the quads to a glamorous photo shoot. Elsewhere, Leanne learns of Tracy's secret fling, but it remains to be seen whether she tells Steve(n)

programme.title: Bradley Walsh & Son: Breaking Dad
programme.desc: The actor Bradley Walsh and his son Barney return for a second series as they embark on another road trip across America together, beginning their journey in Florida, the southernmost point of the country. From there, the pair head to the Palm Beach International Raceway, before getting up close and personal with sharks(n)

programme.title: Coronation Street
programme.desc: Fiz and Tyrone are questioned about the Christmas Day shootings. Meanwhile, Dev orders Chesney to make a choice, and Tracy's explanation shocks Leanne(n)

programme.title: White House Farm
programme.desc: Six-part drama based on the true story of the mass murder of a family in an Essex farmhouse in August 1985, providing a fresh insight into these tragic events. In the early hours of August 7th 1985, Nevill and June Bamber were shot and killed inside their farmhouse, along with their adoptive daughter, Sheila Caffell, and Sheila's six-year-old twin sons, Daniel and Nicholas Caffell. Police receive a call from the only surviving member of June and Nevill's immediate family, their adoptive son Jeremy. At first, it appears to be an open-and-shut case to investigating officer DCI Thomas 'Taff' Jones, but for DS Stan Jones, something does not feel right(n)

programme.title: Holiday Horrors: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Another selection of clips showcasing what can go wrong on holiday, including an unexpected visitor to a couple's tent on their safari holiday, footage from a GoPro camera lost at sea, and a man who took a flight to the edge of space(n)

programme.title: Tenable
programme.desc: Warwick Davis hosts the quiz, in which a family of give answer questions about top 10 lists then try to score a perfect 10 in the final round to take home the prize money. Will they have what it takes to head home with the £125,000 cash?(n)

programme.title: Through the Keyhole
programme.desc: Comedian and matchmaking game show host Paddy McGuinness, MasterChef's Gregg Wallace, and former Gogglebox star and one-time Queen of the Jungle Scarlett Moffatt form the panel this week, as Keith Lemon ventures unbidden into another three mystery celebrities' homes. Keith explores the residences pointing out tell-tale clues, before challenging his trio of stars to guess 'who habitates in a house like this'(n)

programme.title: Tonight
programme.desc: Julie Etchingham explores the growing issue of 'cyber-stalking'. She asks what can be done to prevent technology being used in this way and talks to cyber-crime experts to find out how easy it is for an abuser to take control of their partner's technology(n)

programme.title: Emmerdale
programme.desc: Dawn is blind-sided, Moira receives some harsh truths, and Sarah has a plan(n)

programme.title: Gino's Italian Escape
programme.desc: Gino D'Acampo's latest leg begins in the Italian fashion capital of Milan, where he visits an elegant cocktail bar who were the first to introduce the idea of the aperitivo in the city. Its train station is one of the busiest in Europe and it's from here he travels on to the town of Treviglio, the home of Bianchi bikes - the first mass-manufactured bicycle in the world. Gino gets a tour of the factory before test riding one of their latest electric bikes alongside a group of local cyclists. Moving on, he visits the medieval town of Bergamo, where he meets ice-cream maker Niccolo, and visits a co-operative restaurant(n)

programme.title: Welcome to HMP Belmarsh with Ross Kemp
programme.desc: The documentary maker goes inside the walls of HMP Belmarsh, the country's most notorious maximum-security jail, which has housed the country's most dangerous - and infamous - convicts, including Ian Huntley, Ronnie Biggs and Charles Bronson. Ross explores how staff cope with high-profile inmates, extremists and common criminals living side-by-side and goes inside the High Security Unit, where he meets Muhammad Asif Hafeez, known as Sultan, the alleged mastermind of a drug-smuggling empire(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: Behind the scenes at the airport. Heavy fog throws Heathrow into turmoil and new terminal manager Callum is faced with hundreds of angry and stranded passengers stuck overnight at the airport, while visibility has dropped to less than 100m on the airfield and safety officer Chris is forced to marshal planes. Manager Nilam reveals the secrets of one of Heathrow's most exclusive first-class lounges and dispatcher James and his team hunt around the terminal for late passengers for a flight to Pakistan(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank's role as a witness brings back past horrors, while Michael makes a public accusation. The Windsors greet a long-awaited visitor and the villagers discuss changing a name(n)

programme.title: Classic Emmerdale
programme.desc: Frank and Kim hold a dinner party. Eileen Pollock receives an unwelcome visitor. The Windsors' guest seems determined to stay and Jessica is in trouble after her night out.(n)

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.title: George and Mildred
programme.desc: George decides to place his father in a residential home, but the old man would rather stay with his son. Brian Murphy and Yootha Joyce star(n)

programme.title: George and Mildred

programme.title: Man About the House

programme.title: Heartbeat

programme.desc: Curly is relieved when Andy agrees that Steve must go, Liz asks Steve why he humiliated Jim, and Raquel is thrilled for Tanya that Alex has left his wife(n)

Gerry adds to his growing menagerie, while Margo is emotionally wounded - but gets little sympathy from Leslie(n)

Crime drama, starring Michael Kitchen and Anthony Howell, with Charles Dance and Maggie Steed(n)

programme.title: Midsomer Murders
programme.desc: When Bentham DeQuetteville falls to his death from a roof after seeing a headless horseman, his aristocratic family seem more concerned about their forthcoming Civil War re-enactment than the incident. As Barnaby and Jones try to discover the truth behind the ghostly figure, their investigation uncovers shocking secrets about the DeQuettevilles. Drama, starring Neil Dudgeon and Jason Hughes, with James Callis, William Gaunt and Raquel Cassidy(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: Geordie suspects foul play when computer laboratory head Professor Simms keels over and dies from mercury poisoning. It seems the deceased recently spent the night in the lab with someone other than his wife, possibly a fellow educated man. Geordie seeks advice from Leonard, and the pair start to uncover the surprisingly messy love lives of Simms' colleagues. Elsewhere, Cathy is brutally tricked, Will returns to Grantchester and Mrs C makes a shocking discovery. Drama, starring Robson Green and Tom Brittney(n)

programme.title: Grantchester
programme.desc: A relaxed Will is installed as the new vicar, though is only just back at the vicarage when Geordie drops by requesting his help. Initially reluctant to be drawn in, Will is swayed when he learns that the matter concerns Adam, a profoundly deaf boy from a devout family, whose mother has recently died following a fall. Adam's father thinks his son was responsible and the truth needs uncovering to help the family heal. Will's own troubled past threatens to colour his view of the case - and Geordie realises his new friend has demons of his own. Drama, starring Robson Green and Tom Brittney(n)

programme.title: Agatha Christie's Poirot

Drama, guest starring Kika Markham(n)

programme.desc: A Marine dies in what seems to be a tragic accident during training and the autopsy indicates drowning, but Quincy's second opinion points to a more complex case of murder(n)
