programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A music teacher asks Holmes to discover the identity of a mysterious bearded cyclist who often follows at a distance as she makes her way to work. Period crime drama, guest starring Barbara Wilshere, Michael Siberry and John Castle(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur acquires an old police coach and launches a sight-seeing business. However, it is not long before the vehicle breaks down and his passengers are forced to seek refuge at the Winchester. Drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie is captured and held hostage in a vicarage by four German terrorists - and as time runs out, he finds himself forced to make a deal with them before anyone gets hurt. Gabrielle Drake guest stars, with Lewis Collins, Martin Shaw and Gordon Jackson(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Two villains rob an art dealer of a valuable Goya painting, which they plan to sell in Amsterdam. Regan deduces the dealer's female assistant is an accomplice, and after leaning on her sets off to track down the duo. Seventies crime drama, starring John Thaw(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: Investigations into the death of a construction worker draw Quincy to a landfill site near the small town of Rosewood, where he discovers that illegally dumped pesticides are poisoning the populace. Jack Klugman and Garry Walberg star(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A murderer plays twisted mind games on the emotionally fragile woman who saw him disposing of the body, in the hope of driving her to suicide. Guest starring William Katt (Carrie)(n)

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A distinguished Army colonel is found dead in mysterious circumstances and the finger of suspicion points to his widow. Holmes has to solve a murder involving a locked room with no key, a domestic quarrel and odd animal-like tracks outside the window. Drama, starring Jeremy Brett and David Burke(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: When Ray's van breaks down, Arthur takes to the water using a barge to transport a cargo of dodgy video recorders. However, the fearsome Bible-quoting Welshman who owns them is on his tail. George Cole and Gary Webster star(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are asked to investigate an incident dating back to 1953 after a death-bed confession reveals the key witness in a major corruption trial did not commit suicide as was believed, but was murdered by the police officers assigned to protect her. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: Regan's informer Popeye is abducted after passing on information about a switch of some stolen diamonds. Working with Carter, the DI must catch the villains while protecting their source. Roy Kinnear guest stars, with John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special

programme.title: Jeremy Wade's Mighty Rivers
programme.desc: There appears to be evidence that some of the world's most iconic rivers are in declining health, prompting biologist, extreme angler and naturalist Jeremy Wade to investigate the disappearance of freshwater giants from the planet's largest waterways. Here, he heads to the River Danube in search of two giant beasts - a fish so valuable criminals are hunting it out of existence and a man-sized predator that has become the stuff of dreams. What does the fate of these creatures say about the health of Europe's second longest river?(n)

programme.title: Lethal Weapon
programme.desc: Action drama based on the film franchise. Grief-stricken after the loss of his wife and unborn child, ex-Navy Seal-turned-detective Martin Riggs moves to California to start afresh at the LAPD. He's paired up with veteran detective Roger Murtaugh, who's just coming back to the job after a near-fatal heart attack. Riggs' penchant for diving headfirst into the line of fire soon clashes with Murtaugh's prudent by-the-book technique and his life-on-the-edge attitude makes him a priority case for the department's psychologist. Clayne Crawford and Damon Wayans star(n)

programme.title: Kojak

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A distinguished Army colonel is found dead in mysterious circumstances and the finger of suspicion points to his widow. programme.title: Minder

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Bodie and Doyle are asked to investigate an incident dating back to 1953 after a death-bed confession reveals the key witness in a major corruption trial did not commit suicide as was believed, but was murdered by the police officers assigned to protect her. Drama, starring Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney

programme.title: Quincy ME

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: A detective with a drink problem is demoted to a beat cop, but his determination to make amends for his past mistakes leads him to get in the way of Theo's murder investigation(n)

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A woman hires Holmes and Watson to investigate the strange death of her sister, whose enigmatic final words seemingly hold the key to the case. Period drama, starring Jeremy Brett and David Burke(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur's plans to rig the Winchester's quiz night go awry when the questionmaster, an escaped convict, surrenders himself to the police officers present after suffering a turn. Comedy drama, starring George Cole and Gary Webster(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: Doyle is approached by a woman desperate to prove her imprisoned father is innocent of murder. As he proceeds, the case causes him to become plagued by doubt and believe he sent an innocent man to jail for the murder of his former partner. Martin Shaw, Lewis Collins and Gordon Jackson star(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: An armed gang kidnaps a telephone engineer and uses his expertise to remotely trigger break-in alarms in various banks. While police are diverted to the affected premises, the crooks target other banks. Regan is offended when a telecommunications expert from Bristol CID is drafted in to assist him and Carter with the investigation. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: Junk and Disorderly
programme.desc: Vintage restorers Henry Cole and Sam Lovegrove challenge themselves to make money out of buying and selling other people's unwanted automotive junk and classic collectibles. They begin at Bristol Classic Car Show, where they acquire a quirky car from the 1970s, but making a quick profit does not prove easy(n)

programme.title: Billy Connolly's Ultimate World Tour
programme.desc: The comedian looks back on 25 years of travelogues from his latest destination - his new home in Florida. The film blends archive footage of Billy's globetrotting adventures with interviews as he settles into his new life in the US, going fishing at dawn, meeting an alligator caller in the Everglades and enjoying time with his wife Pamela(n)

programme.title: Carlito's Way (1993)
programme.desc: A former gangster is released from jail and vows to go straight, hoping to earn enough money to escape his New York roots - but his misguided loyalty to a crooked lawyer threatens to drag him back into a life of crime. Brian De Palma's thriller, starring Al Pacino, Sean Penn, Penelope Ann Miller, John Leguizamo and Viggo Mortensen(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo breaks up a bar fight that was instigated by the son of a crime lord. The humiliated aspiring mobster wants the detective dead, and defies his father to put out a contract(n)

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A valuable gem is stolen from the Countess of Morcar, leading the famous detective into a bizarre investigation involving a recovered Christmas goose. Mystery, starring Jeremy Brett, David Burke and Ken Campbell(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur buys a hot-air balloon and attempts to break into aerial advertising. However, he is later abducted by a psychotic business rival who takes him to a disused power station, leaving Ray with the puzzle of how to track him down. George Cole and Gary Webster star(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A stakeout in a bowling alley escalates into a full-scale alert when an atom bomb is discovered, primed and ready to detonate. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star in an instalment featuring a fleeting early appearance by Pamela Stephenson(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The university branch of a bank is robbed, and though Regan and the Flying Squad catch most of the gang a known killer and his sidekick remain inside holding the manager and two women hostage. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Nigel Havers, Melinda Messenger, Michelle Hardwick and Dave Gorman pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: The airport's challenge in dealing with the 100,000 passengers that depart every day. At Terminal 4, Passenger Experience manager Numan has to deal with 40 angry customers after a flight to New York is cancelled, with the travellers demanding to be rebooked immediately. One of the busiest departures of the week is the flight to Dhaka in Bangladesh, and customer service agents Carrie-Anne and Selina bravely step up to the plate in order to get the passengers boarded and the plane away on time(n)

programme.title: Passenger 57 (1992)
programme.desc: After his wife is shot dead during a robbery, a cop hands in his badge and takes up a job offer from an old friend to be a security adviser for a major transatlantic airline. On the flight taking him to his new employers in Los Angeles is an international terrorist being escorted to trial by the FBI - while the prisoner's accomplices are disguised as passengers and crew and plan to hijack the plane. Action thriller, with Wesley Snipes, Bruce Payne, Tom Sizemore, Alex Datcher, Bruce Greenwood and Elizabeth Hurley(n)

Regan is offended when a telecommunications expert from Bristol CID is drafted in to assist him and Carter with the investigation. Police drama, starring John Thaw and Dennis Waterman(n)

programme.title: Quincy ME
programme.desc: Part two of two. Quincy finds himself caught up in a race against time to clear Marty Herrera and unmask the real murderer of the unscrupulous Dr Colella. American drama, starring Jack Klugman and Garry Walberg(n)

programme.title: Kojak
programme.desc: Theo breaks up a bar fight that was instigated by the son of a crime lord. The humiliated aspiring mobster wants the detective dead, and defies his father to put out a contract(n)

programme.title: The Adventures of Sherlock Holmes
programme.desc: A valuable gem is stolen from the Countess of Morcar, leading the famous detective into a bizarre investigation involving a recovered Christmas goose. Mystery, starring Jeremy Brett, David Burke and Ken Campbell(n)

programme.title: Minder
programme.desc: Arthur buys a hot-air balloon and attempts to break into aerial advertising. However, he is later abducted by a psychotic business rival who takes him to a disused power station, leaving Ray with the puzzle of how to track him down. George Cole and Gary Webster star(n)

programme.title: The Professionals
programme.desc: A stakeout in a bowling alley escalates into a full-scale alert when an atom bomb is discovered, primed and ready to detonate. Martin Shaw and Lewis Collins star in an instalment featuring a fleeting early appearance by Pamela Stephenson(n)

programme.title: The Sweeney
programme.desc: The university branch of a bank is robbed, and though Regan and the Flying Squad catch most of the gang a known killer and his sidekick remain inside holding the manager and two women hostage. John Thaw and Dennis Waterman star(n)

programme.title: The Chase Celebrity Special
programme.desc: Bradley Walsh presents as celebrity contestants Nigel Havers, Melinda Messenger, Michelle Hardwick and Dave Gorman pit their wits against ruthless quiz genius the Chaser in the hope of winning a potential prize pot worth thousands of pounds. They must work as a team and play strategically to answer general knowledge questions against the clock and race down the game board to the exit without being caught(n)

programme.title: Heathrow: Britain's Busiest Airport
programme.desc: The airport's challenge in dealing with the 100,000 passengers that depart every day. At Terminal 4, Passenger Experience manager Numan has to deal with 40 angry customers after a flight to New York is cancelled, with the travellers demanding to be rebooked immediately. One of the busiest departures of the week is the flight to Dhaka in Bangladesh, and customer service agents Carrie-Anne and Selina bravely step up to the plate in order to get the passengers boarded and the plane away on time(n)

programme.title: Passenger 57 (1992)
programme.desc: After his wife is shot dead during a robbery, a cop hands in his badge and takes up a job offer from an old friend to be a security adviser for a major transatlantic airline. On the flight taking him to his new employers in Los Angeles is an international terrorist being escorted to trial by the FBI - while the prisoner's accomplices are disguised as passengers and crew and plan to hijack the plane. Action thriller, with Wesley Snipes, Bruce Payne, Tom Sizemore, Alex Datcher, Bruce Greenwood and Elizabeth Hurley(n)

programme.title: Britain's Parking Hell
programme.desc: The daily battles for parking spaces across Britain. Cameras return to Weston-super-Mare to see whether the civil traffic enforcement officers have managed to turn the tide of public opinion since they were introduced two years ago. An angry parker and his brother turn the air blue in Edinburgh as they try to find a space for Burns Night, while a bodybuilder enrages his girlfriend and angry parents by parking in a child-and-baby space, arguing he is too big to squeeze out of his car in a standard bay(n)

programme.title: Red Arrows Take America
programme.desc: Series following the famous squadron as they head to north America for one of their most ambitious foreign tours, performing in front of millions across the United States and Canada, as they fly over iconic landmarks including New York City, Niagara Falls and the Golden Gate Bridge. The trip has been planned in meticulous detail for over a year, but each leg still comes with serious risks and difficulties, from bad weather to major technical problems(n)

programme.title: World's Most Luxurious Trains
programme.desc: A look at top-end trains, such as South Africa's famous Blue Train. A five-star hotel on wheels, guests pay upwards of £2,600 per ticket for the 40-hour journey from Pretoria to Cape Town, enjoying 24-hour butler service, bespoke linen and a soak in their own private bath. Another South African luxury train, Rovos Rail, offers a game drive. In Russia, the Golden Eagle offer a Trans-Siberian train journey, which crosses nine time zones on its two-week journey. The Presidential Train offers a luxury daytrip through the Portuguese countryside, where guests are served a meal created by a Michelin-starred chef(n)

programme.title: Grime & Punishment: Dirty Britain
programme.desc: Following the hidden army tasked with clearing up the extraordinary seasonal mess, as Britain becomes snowed under from an avalanche of yuletide waste, including discarded trees and presents, and more than 100 million black bin bags. Working in their teams across the country, enforcement officers in Walsall chase down a Christmas Day flytipper, while a festive fatberg leaves sewage busters in London with a foul mess on their hands(n)

programme.title: Criminals: Caught on Camera
programme.desc: Documentary in which journalist Nick Wallis joins police forces around the nation to discover how CCTV and technological advances are helping to bring criminals to justice. Here he exposes the rise in ram raids across the UK, with footage of two masked men using a 4x4 to pull the front off a cash machine at the University of East Anglia in Norwich. Nick also shows how detectives used laser-scanning technology to recreate a crime scene and close in on a gang that terrorised West Yorkshire pub owners in 2013(n)

programme.desc: Return of the documentary following Brits who moved to Spain, swapping the UK for a new life in the sun. The first edition follows drag duo John and Barry, known as 'Dragtastic', who get to work repairing dozens of outfits, while also dealing with their respective day jobs of cooking and teaching. Elsewhere, pensioners Ray and Christine try to adapt to the high summer heat, while ARCH horse charity founder Jill races to the rescue of a neglected white stallion(n)

programme.title: The All-Inclusive: How Do They Do It?
programme.desc: Following groups of holiday makers as they embark on an all-inclusive week in the same resort - the Green family, the West family, and a group of friends from Crewe. In the opening episode, the Green family, who have 17 years' experience of all inclusive holidays, share their tips and tricks to save money and make the most out of what is on offer at their resort. Meanwhile, staff in the resort prepare for their busiest day of the year(n)

programme.title: Millionaire Age Gap Love
programme.desc: Part one of two. The popularity of age-gap relationships among the super-rich, meeting six millionaire couples who are decades apart. Stories include that of 37-year-old Carolyn, who landed a job at 54-year-old multi-millionaire John's insurance company, little knowing that within a year she would be engaged to her boss and promoted to CEO of Mansfield Town football club. Meanwhile, DIY millionaire Craig had his life turned upside down when he won the UK's first Big Brother. But now, aged 48, nothing could have prepared him for his whirlwind romance with 33-year-old Laura(n)

programme.title: Eamonn & Ruth: How the Other Half Lives
programme.desc: Eamonn Holmes and Ruth Langsford explore more extravagant lifestyles with a visit to Monte Carlo, the playground of the glamorous super-rich. They rub shoulders with the likes of former Formula 1 boss Flavio Briatore, who invites the couple into his ultra-exclusive club, and discover just how little $30million will buy in the most crowded and expensive property market on earth. The couple also visit the Monte Carlo Casino and are let loose in a wine cellar estimated to be worth 80million euros(n)

programme.title: 12 Angry Men (1957)
programme.desc: Twelve jurors convene to decide the verdict in a murder trial. While 11 of the group are thoroughly convinced the teenage defendant is guilty, one lone voice of reason refuses to cave in to pressure and passionately argues the case for his innocence. Sidney Lumet's legal drama, starring Henry Fonda, Lee J Cobb, Ed Begley, Martin Balsam and Jack Klugman(n)

programme.title: The Professionals (1966)
programme.desc: A wealthy Texan hires four mercenaries to rescue his wife, who has been abducted and taken to Mexico by a bandit gang. However, when they find her, she refuses to leave and they have to take her by force - ending in a cross-border chase with the outlaws on their trail. Western adventure, with Burt Lancaster, Lee Marvin, Robert Ryan, Woody Strode, Jack Palance and Claudia Cardinale(n)

programme.title: The Million Pound Note (1954)
programme.desc: An eccentric multimillionaire bets his equally rich brother that if a man is known to be wealthy, he can obtain everything he needs on credit. To put this theory to the test, they give a £1million note to a penniless seaman, but instruct him never to spend it. Comedy adapted from a story by Mark Twain, starring Gregory Peck, Jane Griffiths, Joyce Grenfell and Wilfrid Hyde White(n)

programme.title: The Tin Star (1957)
programme.desc: A world-weary bounty hunter, formerly a sheriff, arrives in a small town where a newly-appointed lawman is struggling to keep the peace. When he learns of the mercenary's past, the inexperienced lawman seeks his help in learning the tricks of the trade so he can bring an outlaw gang to justice. Anthony Mann's Western, starring Henry Fonda, Anthony Perkins, Lee Van Cleef, Betsy Palmer and Michel Ray(n)

programme.title: Percy Jackson: Sea of Monsters (2013)
programme.desc: The modern-day teenage son of the Greek god Poseidon must lead his friends on a perilous journey into the Bermuda triangle. They need to recover the legendary Golden Fleece to save the world, but getting to it means braving all manner of terrifying monsters. Fantasy adventure sequel, starring Logan Lerman and Alexandra Daddario(n)

programme.title: Stratton (2017)
programme.desc: A British Special Boat Service commando tracks down an international terrorist cell. Thriller, starring Dominic Cooper, Austin Stowell and Gemma Chan(n)

programme.title: Sisters (2015)
programme.desc: Two estranged sisters are reunited when their parents decide to sell the house they grew up in. They return to the family home to clear out their childhood bedrooms, but with their parents away, seize the chance to relive their teenage years and throw one last wild house party. Comedy, starring Amy Poehler, Tina Fey and Dianne Wiest(n)

programme.title: Brakes (2016)
programme.desc: Comedy outlining the seemingly random and fragile nature of the connections that can lead to people becoming part of a couple, showing the final stages of a number of relationships then flashing back to how they got together. Julian Barratt, Kerry Fox and Paul McGann star(n)

programme.title: The Tall Stranger (1957)
programme.desc: A Union soldier struggles to recover from a violent encounter with a gang of lawless cattle rustlers, but his recuperation is cut short as he works to ensure the safety of a group of Confederate homesteaders journeying north at the end of the Civil War. Western based on a novel by Louis L'Amour, starring Joel McCrea, Virginia Mayo, Michael Ansara and Michael Pate(n)

programme.title: Only the Valiant (1951)
programme.desc: A tough cavalry officer selects six unlikely comrades to undertake a vital mission to defend an isolated fort against marauding Apaches. His men's initial hostility soon turns to respect, however, as they realise his wily ways will see them through the conflict. Western adventure based on a novel by Charles Marquis Warren, starring Gregory Peck, Barbara Payton, Ward Bond and Lon Chaney Jr(n)

programme.title: The Last Wagon (1956)
programme.desc: A mixed-race renegade is convicted for the murder of his wife's killers and transported to prison in a wagon train. When the party is attacked by Apaches, the only survivors are the prisoner and a group of orphaned children, so he takes responsibility for leading them to safety. Western, with Richard Widmark, Felicia Farr, Ray Stricklyn and Susan Kohner(n)

programme.title: Blanche Fury (1948)
programme.desc: A penniless governess is invited to stay with a wealthy relative, where she hopes to secure her future and take over the estate by marrying her infatuated cousin. However, her secret romance with a stablehand has tragic consequences for all concerned. Drama, starring Valerie Hobson, Michael Gough, Stewart Granger and Walter Fitzgerald(n)

programme.title: Akeelah and the Bee (2006)
programme.desc: A clever girl from a poor neighbourhood finds school frustrating and tries to hide her intelligence from her classmates. When she competes in a spelling competition, she is surprised to win and with the aid of a strict coach, sets out to take part in a national tournament and provide her community with something to rally round. Drama, starring Keke Palmer, Laurence Fishburne and Angela Bassett(n)

programme.title: Transformers: The Last Knight (2017)
programme.desc: The shape-changing robots and their human allies face a confrontation with their original creator, who has brainwashed their leader and intends to save her own home planet by destroying Earth. To save the human race they must recover an ancient artefact from Arthurian legend - but their mission is threatened by old enemies as they uncover a terrible secret about the history of the world. Sci-fi adventure sequel, starring Mark Wahlberg, Anthony Hopkins, Gemma Chan, Stanley Tucci and John Turturro(n)

programme.title: The Wolf of Wall Street (2013)
programme.desc: A New York stockbroker rises from unemployment to become one of the richest men in the city's financial elite through an illegal scheme to defraud investors. His wealth brings him an outrageously debauched lifestyle, but comes at a price as the FBI become suspicious of his manipulation of the markets. Martin Scorsese's fact-based drama, starring Leonardo DiCaprio, Jonah Hill and Matthew McConaughey(n)

programme.title: The Black Tent (1956)
programme.desc: A wounded British Army captain is saved by a tribe of nomads in the Libyan desert during the Second World War, and they take him to their camp, where he is cared for by the sheikh's daughter and falls in love with her. Romantic drama, with Anthony Steel, Anna-Maria Sandri, Donald Sinden, Andre Morell and Donald Pleasence(n)

programme.title: Hondo (1953)
programme.desc: A hard-boiled cavalry scout with a feral dog as companion stumbles across a woman and her child stranded on their homestead deep inside hostile territory. Western, starring John Wayne, Geraldine Page, Ward Bond and Michael Pate(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

programme.title: Four in a Bed

However, Robert and Sylvia are shocked to find a dirty towel in a drawer, while Emmanuel and Eileen cannot believe the levels of dust and Connie is horrified by the carpet(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed

Robert and Sylvia from the Golden Fleece try to defend their breakfast disaster, but also pull Eileen and Emmanuel up on their low score(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Chester's Penguin Island is home to one of the most enduring love stories in the zoo. At 22, Rud is the oldest on the island, and has been with his partner, Spike, for over 15 years. However, keepers are concerned that Rud's arthritic hip could be affecting his quality of life. Meanwhile, the Sumatran tigers are upgrading to a luxury new enclosure on the other side of the zoo as part of Chester's huge expansion. But they've lived in their home for most of their lives so it's not going to be an easy move(n)

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet newlyweds Nikki and Andy Taylor-Myers in Northampton, who have recently merged households and are unhappy in their cluttered terrace. Property services manager Andy is desperate to stay in their home, but drama and support teacher Nikki has reached boiling point and wants a fresh start(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Countdown
programme.desc: Stuart Maconie takes on James O'Brien, with special guest Rufus Hound joining Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner and Rachel Riley in charge of the numbers(n)

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau
programme.desc: Dick gets to work on the walled garden in the spring and buys a mini tractor for the job - but it may not fit through the gate. Angel designs another suite, with black walls and mosaic mirrors, and decorates the entire dining room with blossoms so a guest can make a special wedding proposal. The couple also consider the possibility of truffle farming(n)

programme.title: Guy Martin's Spitfire
programme.desc: In May 1940, a Mark 1 Spitfire was shot down and crash-landed on a beach in northern France, where it slowly sank into the sand. The wreckage was finally recovered in the 1980s and stored in France for more than 20 years. In this programme, motorcycle racer and mechanic Guy Martin joins a two-year project to rebuild the aeroplane, revealing the engineering and skills involved, and providing a fitting homage to the bravery of everyone involved in its service(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Three boys are admitted for very different reasons, including cyclist Ollie, who arrives with head, neck and facial injuries having been involved in a traffic accident. Cameras also follow Dominic, another 16 year old. He was recovering at home after having his tonsils out when his throat started bleeding and he had to go to hospital(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown
programme.desc: Jimmy Carr hosts the popular quiz as Sean Lock and David O'Doherty compete against Jon Richardson and Natasia Demetriou. John Kearns joins Countdown's resident lexicographer Susie Dent in Dictionary Corner, while Rachel Riley looks after the numbers and letters(n)

programme.title: Dogging Tales
programme.desc: True Stories documentary by photographer Leo Maguire, providing an insight into why men and women engage in or watch sexual activity in front of strangers in public areas after dark. Accompanying his participants to lay-bys, woods and picnic spots around the UK, they discuss their attraction to dogging and reveal how they were introduced to it(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Jasmine Harman and Laura Hamilton help Victor and Alison Dennis, who plan to wind up their IT business to start a B&B, but cannot decide whether they prefer Cornwall or France(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Ceri and Mike Green choose between the Lake District and the French Alps, with Jonnie Irwin showing them three UK properties and Laura Hamilton choosing a trio across the Channel(n)

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig host the third stage of the culinary competition, with judges Paul Hollywood and Prue Leith. Bread Week has arrived, and the remaining bakers face the challenge of proving their worth with Paul's speciality. First, the contestants must prepare a signature bake that is designed to be shared, while the ingredients for a popular summer staple are lurking beneath the gingham cloth in the technical challenge. Finally, an ambitious Showstopper will test their steady hands and artistic flair(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien discover items including baby scales, an oil can and a glass cabinet that are ready for upcycling to turn a profit for their owners(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien hope a clay pigeon trap, a searchlight and a Peugeot 205 will help make a profit for their owners(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at Shannon Slater-Dent and Trevor Tredgett's Bove Town House in Glastonbury, but a poor night's sleep bothers Kathryn and Trevor from Treberthan B&B(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to Treberthan B&B in Par, Cornwall, where owner Kathryn Langley and her best friend Trevor Burnell set out to give their guests a taste of Cornish hospitality(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest continues at SugarBeat Eating House in Swainsthorpe, Norfolk, run by Stevie King and David Lovett, but the establishment's cleanliness quickly comes under fire(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final visit of the week is to the Fritton Arms in Norfolk, run by Ben Davenport and partner Sian Dewaal. David and Stevie are upset about their feedback so they conduct a forensic-style room inspection - complete with rubber gloves - and a paddleboat race on a nearby lake brings out everyone's competitive nature. Ben impresses the guests by revealing the hotel's aristocratic connections - but soon comes under fire for allowing pets to stay(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The owners reunite to find out what they have been paid, with Shannon and Trevor from Bove Town House wanting to discuss Kathryn's comments about her poor night's sleep(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: Rothschild giraffe Orla is about to give birth to her second calf but is three weeks overdue. This episode captures the arrival of the giraffe family's youngest member and its introduction to the rest of the herd. Cameras also follow the Chester team as they attempt to save mountain chicken frogs from extinction, and there's a look at the secret world of three disruptive aardvarks(n)

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer are in Larne in Northern Ireland with Catherine and Nigel Finch, who have been married for seven years, but lately have hit a spot of turbulence due to their dated, 1970s-style bungalow. Nigel quickly fell for their rural location's incredible views, but Catherine has never loved the place and misses having neighbours, so Phil searches for a home that pleases them both, offering room for Nigel's beloved chickens, while giving Catherine more people around her(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Countdown
programme.desc: Sabrina Grant takes on Anna Richardson, with Rufus Hound in Dictionary Corner with Susie Dent. Nick Hewer presents, with Rachel Riley in charge of the numbers(n)

programme.desc: Dick and Angel look ahead to wedding season, with eight couples booking the chateau for their big day. programme.title: The Great Pottery Throw Down
programme.desc: Melanie Sykes hosts as 12 amateur potters compete against one another, with judges Keith Brymer Jones and Sue Pryke asking the contenders to throw a breakfast set and then egg cups(n)

programme.title: My Grandparents' War
programme.desc: Four celebrates actors take deeply personal and immersive journeys to uncover what happened to their grandparents during the Second World War. In the first episode, actress Helena Bonham Carter goes in search of the truth behind her relatives' involvement in the war. Both sets of Helena's grandparents, one set in France and one set in England, were not conventional war heroes as they did not fight in any battles, but she discovers that they did save countless lives during the conflict(n)

programme.title: 24 Hours in A&E
programme.desc: Richard, who's 47, has fallen off a ladder while he was fixing his roof, and 87-year-old Doreen arrives in resus suffering from a serious infection. Elsewhere, doctors also treat five-year-old Cojo, who has come to paediatrics with his dad after injuring his elbow while he was wrestling with his brother(n)

programme.title: 8 Out of 10 Cats Does Countdown

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Nick and Natalie Hammond are selling their family home in Hertfordshire and want to buy somewhere near the coast with more outdoor space for their four children. Nick's keen on a move to Majorca, having holidayed in Spain as a child, but Natalie would prefer to move to Cornwall. They have a cash budget of £275,000 if they buy in Majorca where Laura Hamilton is on hand to help, or £375,000 if they buy in Cornwall, where Jonnie Irwin tries to beat Laura's properties(n)

programme.title: A Place in the Sun: Home or Away
programme.desc: Teenage sweethearts Laura and Stuart Queen have lived in the same house in Carlisle for 16 years but now want a change of location and lifestyle. Stuart wants to move to central Scotland, close to family and work. Horse-lover Laura thinks they'll get more property and land in Murcia in Spain. Jonnie Irwin tries to please them both with a budget of £150,000 in central Scotland and Laura Hamilton tempts them with properties in Spain for a budget of £120,000(n)

programme.title: The Great British Bake Off
programme.desc: Noel Fielding and Sandi Toksvig host Dairy Week, with Prue Leith and Paul Hollywood judging the baker's efforts. The first of the three challenges is a decorative signature bake that involves a tricky turn-out. The bakers then move onto the technical challenge, which is a complex recipe with a long history that dates back to Henry VIII. With the first day behind them, the contestants regroup for the final challenge of the week - an intricate showstopper usually found at the centre of Indian weddings(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel to East Sussex in their hunt to discover valuable items 'lost' in people's homes, with their finds including some Victorian street lamps(n)

programme.title: Find It, Fix It, Flog It
programme.desc: Experts Henry Cole and Simon O'Brien travel Britain to find, fix and flog unwanted possessions on behalf of their owners, turning items into hard cash. A butter churn, a slot machine and a set of print trays are among the finds in this edition(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contest begins at the Richard III in Middleham, North Yorkshire, a pub run by Ken Ward and his wife Sharon, who become suspicious when one couple check out before breakfast. The village is known for its horse racing links, but one of the competitors, Matt Wilson, has a fear of horses, and another couple are concerned at staying above a noisy pub(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The second visit is to the Court House Unique Boutique B&B in Shelsley Walsh, Worcestershire, where hosts Keith and Lisa Shearin hope to impress. On arrival, Ken and Sharon are blown away by their honeymoon suite and the seemingly low price. Suspicions over price fixing drive Matt and Paul to the internet to verify the room rate, where they discover an anomaly, and despite Keith's best efforts at breakfast, Jo and Louis dismiss it as being too fancy(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The contestants travel to Castley Camp in Otley, West Yorkshire, for the third day of the contest, owned by single mum Jo Stevenson and her son Louis. Some of the guests are horrified by stains on their bedding and dust in their room, while an afternoon boot camp fitness session reveals Keith's competitive side. At feedback, Jo is left fuming over the comments about cleanliness and breakfast(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The final two days see the contestants staying the night at the Lion & Swan in Congleton, Cheshire, run by owner Paul Whyle and manager Matt Wilson. A dirty bathroom and a waterbed cause upset, while some guests are still fuming about previous negative feedback. However, the worst is left for breakfast, when Sharon reveals she has discovered their rooms online at a higher price than they were told, upsetting Keith and Lisa and leaving the hosts determined to clear their names(n)

programme.title: Four in a Bed
programme.desc: The B&B owners meet for the last time to find out what they've been paid and to settle some scores. First up are Ken and Sharon from the Richard III, who want to learn more about what's been causing Jo and Louis to lose sleep. Next, Keith and Lisa from the Court House Unique Boutique B&B want to challenge Matt about the allegations of price fixing and vehemently fight their corner. Needless to say, there is tension in the air and axes to grind, before the winner is revealed(n)

programme.title: The Secret Life of the Zoo
programme.desc: The Hi Way elephants have been hit with a potentially deadly virus, with the youngest members of the family most at risk, and the keepers and Chester Zoo's veterinary team do all they can to combat the mysterious illness. Meanwhile, Mac the stallion zebra is introduced to the zoo's three mares in the hope they will produce much needed offspring for this endangered species. There's also a look into the bizarre world of the naked mole rat(n)

programme.title: Kirstie and Phil's Love It or List It
programme.desc: Kirstie Allsopp and Phil Spencer meet Amber and Martin Brammah in Waterbeach, Cambridgeshire, who are undecided on whether they should sell their home or completely renovate it. Amber owns a fitness company and runs classes from home, and feels a bigger property would be better for her business. However, Martin feels that it would be better to use their budget to renovate their current family home(n)

programme.title: Celebrity Countdown

programme.title: Escape to the Chateau

programme.title: Grand Designs
programme.desc: Kevin McCloud meets Andy Stakes and Jeanette Hardy, who are building a new house on top of a sheer cliff on the west coast of Scotland. The pair want to spend £250,000 on a two-bedroom, glass-fronted and earth-sheltered home for themselves and their two beloved Newfoundland dogs. However, construction on such an exposed site is fraught with danger and the pair face no end of problems throughout the build, and a last minute design change that begs the biggest question: even if Andy finishes the house, will Jeanette actually like it?(n)

programme.desc: Max bonds with the patients undergoing chemotherapy with him but a conversation leads him to struggle with the reality of his situation. Reynolds has difficulty handling a personal matter regarding a patient's family and Frome battles with a revelation about his daughter(n)

programme.desc: Across the UK, around 2,200 women go into labour every day. Most make it to hospital, but for those who cannot, the ambulance service is there to help. programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: Historians Ruth Goodman, Alex Langlands and Peter Ginn continue to relive the golden age of steam and how the Victorian railways created modern Britain. They visit Beamish in County Durham, learning how the rail companies first realised there was money to be made in moving people rather than commodities. Then Peter and Alex live the life of navvies, the men who built the thousands of miles of tracks in all weather conditions, and Ruth finds out what impact the new railroads had on the cottage industries. She also learns about the role of the guard, and the trio learn what precautions were necessary for taking a long journey on a train that was yet to include the necessary facilities(n)

programme.title: Full Steam Ahead
programme.desc: The historians take a trip on the Flying Scotsman, arguably the most famous locomotive in the world, along its original route connecting London and Edinburgh - the two most important financial capitals of the Empire. Peter Ginn explores how the railways revolutionised the delivery of mail across Britain, he and Alex Langlands learn about the first electronic communication service - more than 100 years before the arrival of email - and Ruth finds out about the role of the wheeltapper. Plus, why do some railway clocks have more than one minute hand?(n)

programme.title: Impossible Engineering
programme.desc: The construction of Kansai International Airport upon the largest man-made island in the world, in Osaka Bay, Japan. Pioneering innovators from the past such as Pierre Danel, who developed a system against the pounding sea, and 19th-century mechanic Richard Dudgeon made the project possible. The development of airports including Berlin's Tempelhof Airport and London's Croydon Airport are also explored to reveal how engineers attempted this feat of engineering.(n)

programme.desc: Tony Robinson heads to Guernsey and Jersey to tell the story of the islands' surrender to the Nazis during the Second World War. In Guernsey, he hears of the first bombing raid that heralded the German troops' arrival and sees the spot where the first-ever British commando landed in secret, only to see the planned revenge raid fail spectacularly. A ferry then takes him on to Jersey, where he finds almost all of its beaches have a bunker or a machine gun post left over from the conflict(n)

programme.title: Forbidden History
programme.desc: Jamie Theakston travels to Sardinia to meet the scientists and archaeologists who have evidence that suggests giants once existed. Researchers have discovered abnormally large human skeletons in networks of elaborate caves, mines and tombs in Sardinia, China, the United States, northern India and South America. programme.title: Doctors In The War Zone
programme.desc: The United Nations estimates almost 200,000 people have been killed since Syria's civil war began, with many hundreds of thousands more injured. Millions have been forced from their homes, but the full human cost of the conflict is little known to the outside world because of the danger Western journalists face in trying to enter areas not controlled by the government. Most doctors have fled from those locations and many hospitals have been destroyed, but this documentary follows British trauma surgeon David Nott as he makes the perilous journey across the border into Syria to work alongside medics who have stayed behind. He carries out life-saving surgery in a facility forced underground by daily bombardment(n)

programme.desc: Worlds Apart is a fast-paced, in-depth discussion on the pressing issues of the day. The host Oksana Boyko strives to depart from the traditional Q-and-A form of interview in favor of an emotive and engaging conversation. While the title and format of the program presuppose differences of opinion, WA's main goal is to facilitate public debate in an intelligent and respectful manner(n)

programme.title: The Cube
programme.desc: Fitness instructor Nicky Sanford from London and support manager Fay Greaves from Ipswich compete on the game show in which contestants must complete tasks within the confines of a large transparent cube, hoping to walk away with the jackpot of £250,000. programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman mark the impending arrival of the Rio 2016 Olympic Games by inviting Olympians who have previously won gold medals to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. The competing stars are Paralympic discus-thrower Aled Davies and former heptathlete Tessa Sanderson, ex-competitive swimmer David Wilkie and pentathlete Mary Peters, Athens relay stars Jason Gardener and Marlon Devonish, and ex-wheelchair racer Tanni Grey-Thompson and former track and field athlete Sally Gunnell(n)

programme.title: Pointless Celebrities
programme.desc: Alexander Armstrong and Richard Osman challenge four teams of stars from the world of music to take part in the unorthodox general knowledge quiz. Former Marillion lead singer Fish teams up with Scottish folk star Barbara Dickson, while Haircut 100's Nick Heyward joins forces with pop rocker Toyah Willcox. Heather Small, formerly the lead singer of M People, joins the Neasden Queen of Soul Mari Wilson, and Liberty X's Jessica Taylor partners her former bandmate Kelli Young. As ever, the contestants strive to correctly guess the least likely correct answers to a series of questions posed to members of the public(n)
